findings.topic: Instagram story-like bot for targeted audience engagement
findings.title: Automated story-like bot targets competitor audiences at 10k interactions/day
findings.claim: Running automated story-like bots against a competitor creator's follower list drives highly qualified traffic to your model's IG profile and OF link-in-bio at massive scale.
findings.what_they_do: They use bot accounts (not the model's main IG) to scrape followers from competitor OF creators on Instagram, filter out inactive/no-profile-picture accounts, then auto-like those users' stories at up to 10,000 interactions per bot per day (300k/month per bot, 3M/month with 10 bots). The notification triggers the target to click the bot-account profile, which routes to the model's main IG and then to OF.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented traffic/distribution strategy beyond Publer scheduling and Telegram manual posting (§5 GAP). This is a high-volume, automated top-of-funnel acquisition play Palm has no equivalent for. The competitor explicitly avoids using the model's main account credentials, reducing ban risk.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate third-party IG story-like automation tools (e.g., Jarvee-style services) as a paid traffic layer. Start with one creator, source their 2–3 closest competitor audiences, apply follower-quality filters (active, has profile photo), and measure profile-visit-to-follow and follow-to-OF-click conversion. Cap at volumes below IG's detection thresholds. Document as a repeatable traffic SOP to fill §5 GAP.

sources.quote: have you ever opened up Instagram and gone into your notifications and you've seen that some random account has liked your story that maybe doesn't even follow you and you click onto and it's actually an only fans model
per bot you can do up to 10 000 interactions per day so imagine that's 300 000 across a month

findings.topic: Competitor-audience targeting with quality filtering before bot interactions
findings.title: Filter competitor follower lists to remove bots/inactives before outreach
findings.claim: Maximizing bot ROI requires pre-filtering scraped follower lists to remove accounts with no profile picture, bot signals, or recent creation dates before any interaction.
findings.what_they_do: Before running any bot interaction, they scrape a target competitor's followers, then apply filters: remove no-profile-picture accounts, remove recently created accounts, remove known-bot signals. Only the remaining 'qualified' pool receives story likes, post likes, or DMs. This is framed as maximizing ROI per interaction rather than spraying randomly.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented outbound traffic system at all (§5 GAP). This filtering logic is a best-practice layer on top of any mass-interaction strategy — Palm should adopt it if/when implementing any bot or mass-outreach traffic layer.
findings.recommendation: When Palm implements any mass-IG-outreach tool, build a pre-flight filtering step into the SOP: (1) pull follower list via scraper, (2) filter out no-avatar accounts, (3) filter accounts created <90 days ago, (4) optionally filter accounts with zero posts. Codify these as default filter presets in whatever tooling is chosen.

sources.quote: out of the hundred thousand people some of them will be inactive some of them will be Bots some of them will not have a profile picture and maybe just created the account and so on we can literally filter those out

findings.topic: Comment-like bot for multi-person reach from single interaction
findings.title: Post-comment likes reach ~100 viewers per action vs. 1 for story likes
findings.claim: Automatically liking posts' comments reaches roughly 100 people per action (others viewing the comment thread) versus story likes which reach only the single account holder.
findings.what_they_do: Same audience-sourcing and filtering pipeline as the story-like bot, but the action is liking a post comment rather than a story. The upside: the comment notification and the model's bot-account profile are visible to all other users engaging with that comment thread — estimated ~100 viewers per single like action — multiplying reach per interaction unit.
findings.vs_us: No equivalent at Palm (§5 GAP). This is a force-multiplier variant of IG engagement automation that Palm could layer in alongside story-like automation for higher reach efficiency.
findings.recommendation: When testing IG bot traffic (see story-like finding above), run a parallel A/B: story-like bot vs. comment-like bot on the same filtered audience pool. Measure downstream: profile visits, follows, link-in-bio clicks, OF subs. Use the higher-performing format as primary. Comment likes may be particularly effective on high-engagement posts in male-interest niches.

sources.quote: with the comments compared to the story views is with the story if you view somebody's story or if you even like it that's one action and it can only reach one person... with a comment I mean it's unlimited how many people can see it but typically about 100 people or so

findings.topic: Mass Instagram DM bot for OF link/post distribution
findings.title: Mass IG DM bot sends model's latest posts to filtered prospects at 100k+/month
findings.claim: Sending the model's latest IG post directly to hundreds of thousands of pre-qualified prospects via automated DM drives followers, engagement boosts, and OF click-throughs simultaneously.
findings.what_they_do: After filtering the target audience (same pipeline), the bot sends a DM containing the model's most recent IG post to each prospect. This does three things at once: (1) boosts likes/comments on the post via engagement from curious recipients, (2) drives profile follows, (3) drives link-in-bio clicks to OF. Volume cited: tens of thousands per day, hundreds of thousands per month. The model's own IG credentials are never used — all done via secondary accounts.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no mass-DM traffic layer documented (§5 GAP). This is the highest-volume tactic in the video and the one the creator calls 'most popular.' It is also the highest-risk for secondary account bans. Palm's outsourced chat team handles OF DMs (§8) but this is top-of-funnel IG prospecting, not OF monetization.
findings.recommendation: Treat IG mass-DM as a traffic experiment for one willing real creator. Use aged secondary IG accounts (not the model's main), send a single warm message with her latest post (no explicit OF link in first message to reduce spam flags), and measure conversion. Set volume caps conservatively (500–1,000 DMs/day per secondary account) to test before scaling. Document risk/reward in a platform-risk SOP (§ platform-risk).

sources.quote: with this but this will actually send out Mass messages at scale it would send out tens of thousands and in a month hundreds of thousands of messages in the DM

not only will it boost of course that engagement likes and comments she'll get a ton of followers but of course most importantly they'll click onto the post then click on our profile and go ahead and click on the link in the bio

findings.topic: Credential isolation — bot accounts never use model's main IG login
findings.title: All bot activity runs on secondary accounts; model's main IG credentials never shared
findings.claim: Keeping bot operations entirely on secondary/shadow accounts means the model's primary IG is never at risk of a ban even if bot accounts get flagged.
findings.what_they_do: Across all four bot types (story likes, comment likes, mass DM, repost), the agency explicitly never takes the model's actual IG login or password. All interactions originate from purpose-built secondary accounts operating in the background. This is repeated as a selling point three separate times in the video.
findings.vs_us: Palm already applies a strong credential-isolation and hardware-isolation principle to its AI persona accounts (§3/§4 — GrapheneOS Pixel, 1 SIM/persona, vault-stored creds). However, this practice is not documented for real-creator traffic operations (§5 GAP). The underlying principle is the same; Palm should extend it explicitly to any real-creator outbound bot layer.
findings.recommendation: If Palm adopts any IG bot/automation traffic layer for real creators, codify credential isolation as a non-negotiable rule in the platform-risk SOP: model's primary IG login is NEVER entered into any third-party automation tool. All bot activity uses dedicated secondary accounts with separate Gmail/SIM per account, consistent with Palm's existing AI-account hygiene practices (§3).

sources.quote: we would never need the password of the login detail of your Instagram so it would never actually have to use your model's Instagram account so it will never get banned

findings.topic: Automated cross-platform repost — TikTok/YouTube Shorts to IG, watermark-free
findings.title: Repost bot auto-downloads TikTok/YT Shorts and re-uploads to IG with no watermark
findings.claim: An automated repost bot removes the TikTok watermark and re-uploads the video to Instagram Reels, expanding reach to a second platform with zero manual effort.
findings.what_they_do: When the model posts to TikTok or YouTube Shorts, the bot detects the new video, downloads it (stripping the TikTok watermark automatically), and re-uploads it to Instagram. No login credentials required from the model. This runs fully in the background — set-and-forget. The stated benefit is reaching both the TikTok and IG audiences from a single piece of content.
findings.vs_us: Palm's content pipeline (§1) is IG-first (inspo → film → edit → schedule via Publer). There is no documented cross-platform republishing automation from TikTok to IG. Publer does support multi-platform scheduling, but that requires manual upload to Publer — not automated downstream capture from TikTok. This bot fills a different gap: auto-capturing content the creator posts natively to TikTok.
findings.recommendation: For real creators who self-post to TikTok independently of Palm's pipeline, evaluate tools like SaveTok, Repurpose.io, or similar that auto-detect new TikTok posts, strip watermarks, and push to IG Reels. This recovers content that otherwise bypasses Palm's pipeline and ensures it also benefits the IG funnel. Low effort, low risk — good quick win. Alternatively, enforce that all content goes through Palm's Publer pipeline first (avoiding the need for this bot entirely by controlling upload order).

sources.quote: when your model goes ahead and posts a Tick Tock or even a YouTube short this bot will quite literally download the video and this is the best part with no watermark so we'll download a video and repurpose it and re-upload it onto Instagram

findings.topic: Bot-based automation as an agency service/upsell tier
findings.title: Package automation bots as an upsell service to existing agency clients
findings.claim: Bundling bot services (story likes, comment likes, mass DM, reposter) as a premium add-on or inner-circle perk creates an additional revenue stream for the agency beyond standard revenue share.
findings.what_they_do: The agency offers these bot services to clients via a call-booking funnel (not self-serve). Existing Inner Circle / consulting clients get access for free as a retention perk. New clients book a qualification call before being granted access — controlling supply and adding perceived exclusivity. This creates a two-tier model: standard management + premium automation tier.
findings.vs_us: Palm operates on a revenue-share commission model (§7) with no documented upsell tiers or add-on services. Palm's current offering is full-service management. This competitor is carving out a productized automation layer that can be sold separately — potentially to creators Palm doesn't fully manage, or as an upsell to existing creators to accelerate their growth.
findings.recommendation: Explore whether Palm could offer a 'traffic acceleration' add-on (fixed monthly fee for IG bot traffic services) as a complement to the core rev-share deal. This would (a) add a predictable revenue stream, (b) give creators a concrete growth lever Palm controls, and (c) differentiate Palm's pitch vs. agencies offering only content + chat. Requires resolving the platform-risk SOP first. Flag for §7 pricing model discussion.

sources.quote: this is not something that we're just going to go ahead and launch to absolutely everybody... if you are already in Aura Consulting Pro in our Inner Circle in a Discord you already have access to this for free

findings.title: Correct Snapchat profile configuration to avoid pre-ban flagging
findings.claim: Snapchat flags and bans accounts before they even start if the Bitmoji, public profile, bio, and Snapchat Plus subscription are not set up correctly.
findings.what_they_do: Agency follows a specific account setup checklist covering Bitmoji creation, public profile configuration, bio copy, and Snapchat Plus activation before doing any outreach or posting — treating setup as a trust-signal layer that precedes all activity.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented Snapchat traffic strategy (§5 GAP — traffic/distribution not documented). Snapchat is not part of any current distribution playbook.
findings.recommendation: When building out Palm's traffic playbook, treat Snapchat account setup as a prerequisite checklist (Bitmoji, public profile, bio, Snapchat Plus) before any warm-up or posting begins — analogous to Palm's hardware-isolated warmup for AI personas (§3/§4) but applied to Snapchat accounts for real creators.

sources.quote: The Bitmoji, the public profile, the bio, Snapchat Plus. If you get any of that wrong, Snapchat flags you before you even start

findings.title: Staged quick-add schedule to avoid same-day Snapchat ban
findings.claim: Snapchat bans accounts that add too many people too fast, so there is an exact daily add-limit schedule that must be followed and gradually increased.
findings.what_they_do: Agency uses a structured warm-up schedule with specific daily caps on how many users to quick-add, starting very low and incrementally increasing over time — no guessing on volume ramp.
findings.vs_us: Palm has a mature 90-day warmup cadence for AI social accounts (§4) with day-by-day activity rules, but this is IG/TikTok-focused. No equivalent Snapchat warm-up schedule exists. The principle (staged ramp, per-day caps) is directly analogous to Palm's existing warmup methodology.
findings.recommendation: Build a Snapchat-specific warm-up schedule modeled on Palm's existing AI-account warmup structure (§4) — define Day 1–7 add limits, Day 8–14 increase thresholds, etc. The pattern Palm already uses for IG warmup translates directly; just need Snapchat-specific numbers researched and codified.

sources.quote: There's an exact schedule for how many people to add at the start and when to increase it. If you just guess this part, you will be banned.

findings.topic: Snapchat Spotlight for organic fan acquisition
findings.title: Use Snapchat Spotlight posts strategically to drive inbound quick-adds
findings.claim: Posting correctly to Snapchat Spotlight generates organic views and causes target users to quick-add the creator, producing inbound traffic without manual outreach.
findings.what_they_do: Agency has a specific content format/posting method for Spotlight (not just spamming) that grows views and snap score simultaneously, converting Spotlight viewers into quick-adders who then enter the DM funnel.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no Snapchat presence or Spotlight strategy documented (§5 GAP). Palm's content engine (§1) is IG/TikTok Reels-focused. Spotlight is a parallel short-form organic channel that Palm is not utilizing.
findings.recommendation: Investigate Spotlight as an organic top-of-funnel channel alongside IG Reels. Since Palm already has a data-driven content ideation pipeline (§1), the same inspo-scrape → analyze → film workflow could potentially surface Spotlight-optimized content formats. Prioritize learning what content format triggers Spotlight algorithmic distribution.

sources.quote: Spotlight is where the organic traction comes from. But you can't just spam posts. There's a way to do it that gets you views and increases your snap score.

findings.topic: Snap score as social proof signal
findings.title: Grow snap score proactively to prevent target users from flagging account as fake
findings.claim: A low snap score causes potential fans who receive a quick-add to dismiss the account as a bot or fake, killing conversion before any content is seen.
findings.what_they_do: Agency actively builds snap score using a specific method before or during outreach so that when a man receives and checks the account, the score reads as a real, active user — functioning as social proof.
findings.vs_us: Palm's AI-persona warmup (§4) explicitly addresses social proof (posting cadence, stories, engagement activity during warmup before monetization). The same logic applies here to Snapchat snap score. Palm has no Snapchat equivalent of this.
findings.recommendation: Add snap score cultivation as a mandatory warmup metric in any future Snapchat playbook — analogous to how Palm's IG warmup tracks follower growth and engagement rate before enabling OF CTAs. Define a minimum snap score threshold before beginning active quick-add outreach.

sources.quote: Snap score matters more than you think. It affects your visibility and your social proof. Cuz when a guy adds you back and he sees that your snap score is one 2 3 10 whatever, it seems slow. He thinks you're a fake account.

findings.title: Route Snapchat traffic through Telegram as a conversion bridge to OnlyFans
findings.claim: Snapchat traffic must be funneled through a specifically configured Telegram step rather than linking directly to OnlyFans, because direct link-spamming gets accounts banned and reduces conversion.
findings.what_they_do: Agency sets up a Telegram channel/bot as a middle layer between Snapchat stories/DMs and the OnlyFans link — the Snapchat account drives viewers to Telegram, and Telegram is configured to convert them into OF subscribers. Direct OF links in Snapchat are avoided.
findings.vs_us: Palm already uses Telegram operationally (Amin contractor coordination, posting webhooks — §5), but not as a consumer-facing traffic funnel. Palm's current funnel is social → OF directly (§0). This Snapchat → Telegram → OF bridge is a distinct multi-hop architecture Palm has not documented.
findings.recommendation: When building a Snapchat traffic playbook, adopt the Telegram bridge model: Snapchat stories/DMs → Telegram CTA → OF subscribe. Document the Telegram channel setup (e.g., pinned OF link, welcome message, drip content) as a reusable module. This also reduces platform risk of OF link detection on Snapchat.

sources.quote: This is how you actually convert the traffic you're getting. Without it, you're just posting stories for no reason. There's a specific way to set up Telegram so people actually click through and end up subbing to your only fans.
